title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. pseudovulneraria
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. pseudovulneraria, also known as kidney vetch, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. Traditionally, kidney vetch has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Kidney vetch can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically proven.
